req: REQ-015 (Per-Project Comment Thread (full Slack-style channel deferred to v1.5))

issues.detail: The amended fixture checks access only after release and replacement are both complete. An implementation that retains the former volunteer until reassignment would pass, despite membership being limited to the currently assigned volunteer.
issues.fix: Assert immediately after release, before reassignment, that the former volunteer loses read/post access; then separately assert the replacement gains access when assigned.

issues.detail: “An @-mention has no special behavior” merely repeats the requirement and does not define observable pass/fail behavior.
issues.fix: Assert that mention syntax remains literal plain text, creates no profile link or autocomplete resolution, and emits no mention-specific notification.

issues.detail: “When a party posts” can be satisfied with one posting direction and leaves the reciprocal flow—and recipients when an escalated admin participates—untested.
issues.fix: Parameterize the test for NGO-to-volunteer and volunteer-to-NGO posting, subject to REQ-016’s anti-spam guard, and define/test the escalated-admin recipient case.

issues.detail: The amendment calls the notification emitter the “sole system-event writer.” REQ-016 makes it the sole notification writer, not the sole producer or handler of system events; probing that component cannot prove that another path never writes to the thread.
issues.fix: Verify the invariant at the thread write boundary, such as accepting only authenticated user-authored comments, and exercise system-event producers against that boundary.

issues.detail: The payment fixture must appear in AT-010’s activity feed, but REQ-010 requires activity there to be tied to task titles. A project-level payment event has no task title, exposing a new unresolved cross-requirement contradiction.
issues.fix: Escalate this as a new TENSION and, pending clarification, require the payment event’s notification and absence from the thread without assigning it to AT-010’s task-tied activity feed.

issues.detail: The phrase “from the read-only status panel” is only a Given condition. No assertion verifies that the NGO cannot mutate task state, assignment, or task metadata through that panel.
issues.fix: Add explicit task-mutation attempts from the NGO panel and assert rejection/no state change while task-anchored commenting remains available.

issues.detail: The test requires the thread whenever an authorized participant opens the project page, without a lifecycle qualifier. Under REQ-005.5 an assigned volunteer already exists in matched_pending_fuel, but the thread opens only at kickoff.
issues.fix: Constrain the placement test to a thread-bearing project at or after kickoff; do not require thread access during pre-kickoff states.
